skill.name: skill-writer
skill.description: Guide users through creating focused Agent Skills for Claude Code, including SKILL.md frontmatter, structure, examples, validation, and troubleshooting.
skill.summary: Create well-structured Claude Code skills with clear scope, valid frontmatter, examples, and validation steps.
skill.author: pytorch

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows they are Markdown examples, inline paths, and validation guidance in SKILL.md. No prompt injection, malicious intent, data exfiltration, network access, or real cryptographic implementation was found. The skill is safe to publish with low risk because users may copy example shell commands manually.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misidentified as Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported external command detections are documentation examples inside Markdown code fences, not executable skill code. They show users how to create directories, install packages, run helpers, inspect files, and validate scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are inside instructional Markdown sections and are never invoked by a script or runtime hook. The only residual risk is user error when manually copying example commands.

low_findings.title: Documented Hidden Skill Paths Are Expected
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. References to ~/.claude/skills and .claude/skills describe supported personal and project skill locations. They do not read secrets, enumerate arbitrary hidden files, or write outside documented skill directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are the documented locations for Claude Code skills and are used only in setup and troubleshooting examples. No evidence of credential harvesting or broad filesystem traversal was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported lines contain descriptions, examples, checklist text, and frontmatter guidance. No weak hash algorithm, cipher, signing logic, key material, or cryptographic operation appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic API, weak algorithm name, or secret-handling code at the reported lines. The scanner appears to have matched ordinary documentation language.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Checklist Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported reconnaissance lines are general skill-writing guidance and validation checklist items. They do not collect host data, inspect system state, or enumerate user files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language checklist content, not commands or logic that gather system details. No semantic evidence of reconnaissance intent was found.

content.value_statement: Writing reusable AI skills is hard when scope, structure, and triggers are unclear. This skill guides authors through focused SKILL.md files, examples, validation, and troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Helps define a focused purpose and activation triggers for a new skill.
Explains personal and project skill locations for Claude Code.
Shows required SKILL.md frontmatter and naming rules.
Provides example structures for single-file and multi-file skills.
Guides validation, testing, and troubleshooting when a skill does not activate.
limitations: Does not generate or validate skills with a separate executable tool.
Does not guarantee that Claude Code will activate a skill in every context.
Does not inspect installed skills unless the user provides files or asks for file review.
Example commands require normal user judgment before running them.

use_cases.title: Create a Personal Workflow Skill
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated personal workflow into a focused Claude Code skill with clear triggers and setup steps.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Create project skills that follow shared naming, frontmatter, examples, and validation conventions.

use_cases.title: Debug Skill Activation
use_cases.description: Improve descriptions, file locations, and validation checks when Claude Code does not select a skill.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Claude Code skill for [workflow]. Ask me what you need, then draft the SKILL.md structure.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Existing Frontmatter
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SKILL.md frontmatter and improve the name and description so Claude Code can discover it reliably.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Multi-File Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-file skill for [domain]. Include what belongs in SKILL.md, references, examples, scripts, and templates.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Skill Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this skill for scope, activation triggers, frontmatter validity, tool permissions, examples, dependencies, and troubleshooting gaps.

output_examples.input: I need a skill for reviewing pull requests in our repository.
output_examples.output: A concise skill plan with a focused name, trigger-rich description, recommended tool permissions, review steps, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: Claude Code is not using my spreadsheet skill.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering description specificity, file location, YAML frontmatter, trigger phrases, and realistic activation tests.

output_examples.input: Convert this long prompt into a reusable team skill.
output_examples.output: A scoped skill outline that separates quick instructions from supporting references and examples.

best_practices: Keep each skill focused on one clear capability and one expected workflow.
Write descriptions with concrete trigger phrases, file types, and user intents.
Test skill activation with realistic prompts before sharing it with a team.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad skills that combine unrelated workflows.
Do not use vague descriptions that only say the skill helps with a topic.
Do not hide dependencies or required commands from the user.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create Claude Code Agent Skills with valid SKILL.md frontmatter, clear instructions, examples, and validation steps.

faq.question: Can it create personal and project skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains both personal skills under ~/.claude/skills and project skills under .claude/skills.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents example commands that users may choose to run during setup or troubleshooting.

faq.question: Can it fix a skill that does not activate?
faq.answer: It can guide troubleshooting by checking descriptions, trigger words, file location, frontmatter, and validation prompts.

faq.question: Does it support multi-file skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when to use supporting files such as references, examples, scripts, and templates.

faq.question: Is this only for Claude Code?
faq.answer: The guidance is written for Claude Code Agent Skills, but Codex and Claude users can use it to draft skill documentation.
